serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup (approximately 350 ml / 350 g) as typically served at a Singaporean bubble tea or speciality tea outlet.

nutrient_source.title: Passionfruit Green Tea (Less Sugar) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Passionfruit Green Tea (Less Sugar), cr_id D08010081. All per-100g values are as reported.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sugar-sweetened beverages and lightly sweetened tea beverages — analogous estimate based on sucrose GI and beverage matrix
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for passionfruit green tea (less sugar). GI estimated at 55 (low, upper boundary of low range ≤55) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for lightly sweetened tea beverages and sucrose-based drinks with comparable sugar content (~8g/100g). Sucrose GI is 65; dilution in a tea matrix and partial fructose content of passionfruit flavouring moderates the effective GI to approximately 55. Per University of Sydney GI Database classification: low GI = ≤55, medium GI = 56–69, high GI = ≥70. A GI of 55 therefore falls in the low category. GL per serving = (55 × 8.26 × 3.50) / 100 = 15.9 g per 350 ml serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage (8.26 g sugar/100 g) — liquid sugar independently raises blood pressure risk regardless of low sodium content
hypertension.guidance: Despite very low sodium (1 mg/100 g), the 8.26 g/100 g sugar content classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage; AHA 2018 evidence links SSB consumption to elevated blood pressure independent of sodium, and individuals with hypertension should request the genuinely unsweetened (zero-sugar) version or switch to plain green tea.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.26 g sugar/100 g and negligible dietary fibre (0.03 g/100 g) — liquid sugar is absorbed rapidly
type2_diabetes.guidance: The 'less sugar' designation does not remove this beverage from the SSB category; with 8.26 g sugar/100 g and virtually no fibre, liquid glucose and fructose are absorbed rapidly, causing acute postprandial glycaemic excursions, and ADA guidelines recommend avoiding all SSBs including reduced-sugar variants for individuals managing type 2 diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular SSB consumption linked to elevated triglycerides and increased CVD risk; green tea polyphenols (EGCG) provide partial cardioprotective offset
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While green tea catechins (EGCG) have documented cardioprotective properties, the 8.26 g/100 g sugar load in this beverage is associated with elevated triglycerides and increased cardiovascular risk with habitual consumption; individuals with CVD should limit intake and prefer the unsweetened version to retain the cardioprotective benefits of green tea without the sugar burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Residual sugar (8.26 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; insufficient fibre to buffer inflammatory effects
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The reduced but still significant sugar content (8.26 g/100 g) continues to promote inflammatory cytokine activity; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should switch to genuinely unsweetened passionfruit green tea to access EGCG anti-inflammatory benefits without the pro-inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Passionfruit Green Tea (Less Sugar) is a freshly brewed Singaporean speciality tea beverage made from green tea infused with passionfruit flavouring and prepared with a reduced quantity of added sugar compared to the standard version. It is widely available at bubble tea shops and speciality tea outlets across Singapore, typically served chilled or over ice in cup sizes ranging from 350 ml to 700 ml, with optional toppings such as pearls, aloe vera, or grass jelly. The flavour profile combines the light, grassy, slightly astringent character of green tea with the bright, tropical tartness of passionfruit, balanced by a mild residual sweetness. Per 100 g, the beverage provides 43 kcal, 8.26 g carbohydrate (all as sugar), 0.01 g protein, 0.0 g fat, 0.03 g dietary fibre, and 1 mg sodium. Despite the 'less sugar' label, the beverage retains 8.26 g sugar per 100 g, classifying it as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under ADA, AHA, and HPB Singapore guidelines. This is clinically significant for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease, as liquid sugar is absorbed rapidly with minimal fibre buffering. Green tea polyphenols, particularly EGCG, provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, but these benefits are partially offset by the residual sugar content. The glycaemic index is estimated at 55 (low, at the upper boundary of the low GI range per University of Sydney classification) based on analogous lightly sweetened tea beverages. For health-conscious consumers, the genuinely unsweetened version is recommended to fully access the benefits of green tea catechins without the glycaemic burden.

notes: fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (D08010081); this is a water-based brewed tea beverage containing no fat by formulation. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero for the same reason. protein_g is 0.01 g/100 g (trace, not zero). gi_category is 'low': per University of Sydney GI Database classification, low GI = ≤55, medium GI = 56–69, high GI = ≥70; a GI of 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low category. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid profiles for this specific less-sugar passionfruit green tea variant are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D08010081 as of 2026-04-16. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database lightly sweetened tea beverage data; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. GL per 350 g serving = (55 × 8.26 × 3.50) / 100 = 15.9 g.
